verses.text: 'O give thanks unto the LORD; for he is good,' the 'God of gods' and 'Lord of lords,' 'for his mercy endureth for ever.' The refrain begins. His goodness and supremacy are matched by mercy that never ends.

'O give thanks unto the LORD; for he is good,' the 'God of gods' and 'Lord of lords,' 'for his mercy endureth for ever.' The refrain begins. His goodness and supremacy are matched by mercy that never ends.

'O give thanks unto the LORD; for he is good,' the 'God of gods' and 'Lord of lords,' 'for his mercy endureth for ever.' The refrain begins. His goodness and supremacy are matched by mercy that never ends.

He alone does great wonders, made the heavens by wisdom, and stretched the earth above the waters — 'for his mercy endureth for ever.' Creation itself displays His mercy. The God who made all things made them in kindness.

He alone does great wonders, made the heavens by wisdom, and stretched the earth above the waters — 'for his mercy endureth for ever.' Creation itself displays His mercy. The God who made all things made them in kindness.

He alone does great wonders, made the heavens by wisdom, and stretched the earth above the waters — 'for his mercy endureth for ever.' Creation itself displays His mercy. The God who made all things made them in kindness.

He made the great lights, the sun to rule the day and the moon and stars the night — 'for his mercy endureth for ever.' The heavens shine by His mercy. Every dawn and starlit night is a token of His enduring love.

He made the great lights, the sun to rule the day and the moon and stars the night — 'for his mercy endureth for ever.' The heavens shine by His mercy. Every dawn and starlit night is a token of His enduring love.

He made the great lights, the sun to rule the day and the moon and stars the night — 'for his mercy endureth for ever.' The heavens shine by His mercy. Every dawn and starlit night is a token of His enduring love.
He smote Egypt's firstborn and brought Israel out 'with a strong hand' — 'for his mercy endureth for ever.' Redemption flowed from mercy. The God who judged the oppressor freed His people in love.
He smote Egypt's firstborn and brought Israel out 'with a strong hand' — 'for his mercy endureth for ever.' Redemption flowed from mercy. The God who judged the oppressor freed His people in love.
He smote Egypt's firstborn and brought Israel out 'with a strong hand' — 'for his mercy endureth for ever.' Redemption flowed from mercy. The God who judged the oppressor freed His people in love.

He divided the Red Sea, led Israel through, and 'overthrew Pharaoh and his host' — 'for his mercy endureth for ever.' Deliverance and judgment met. The same act that saved His people drowned their enemies, all in mercy.

He divided the Red Sea, led Israel through, and 'overthrew Pharaoh and his host' — 'for his mercy endureth for ever.' Deliverance and judgment met. The same act that saved His people drowned their enemies, all in mercy.

He divided the Red Sea, led Israel through, and 'overthrew Pharaoh and his host' — 'for his mercy endureth for ever.' Deliverance and judgment met. The same act that saved His people drowned their enemies, all in mercy.

He led His people through the wilderness and smote great kings — 'for his mercy endureth for ever.' He guided and defended them. The God who leads His own through deserts conquers all that stands in the way.

He led His people through the wilderness and smote great kings — 'for his mercy endureth for ever.' He guided and defended them. The God who leads His own through deserts conquers all that stands in the way.

He led His people through the wilderness and smote great kings — 'for his mercy endureth for ever.' He guided and defended them. The God who leads His own through deserts conquers all that stands in the way.

He struck down Sihon and Og and gave their land 'for an heritage unto Israel his servant' — 'for his mercy endureth for ever.' He kept His promise. The inheritance of His people was the gift of His mercy.

He struck down Sihon and Og and gave their land 'for an heritage unto Israel his servant' — 'for his mercy endureth for ever.' He kept His promise. The inheritance of His people was the gift of His mercy.

He 'remembered us in our low estate,' redeemed us, and gives food to all flesh; 'O give thanks unto the God of heaven... for his mercy endureth for ever.' His mercy reaches the lowly and feeds all. From creation to redemption to daily bread, every good is His enduring mercy.
